key_insight: An AI-based pathology tool can score ulcerative colitis biopsy histology with pathologist-level consistency and repeatability across standard remission and severity indices.
clinical_relevance: If validated in practice, this could standardize histology assessment in UC trials and reduce reader variability when measuring treatment response.
method: Trained on 10,230 UC biopsy slides using a foundation-model + multiple instance learning approach
Predicted Geboes subgrades, then derived Geboes grade-level score, Robarts Histopathology Index (RHI), and Nancy Histologic Index (NHI)
Benchmarked against consensus pathologist reads on an independent verification set
evidence_signal: On a standalone verification set, the model was non-inferior to 3 pathologists for all seven Geboes subgrades and key endpoints, and it showed >99% repeatability across scoring metrics.
watch_item: Performance was shown on a verification dataset, so broader real-world generalization, workflow integration, and regulatory validation still need confirmation.
paper_title: Standardized, repeatable ulcerative colitis histology scoring and endpoint assessment using an automated, foundation model-based tool
